metadata.title: Distributed Systems Formal Methods: Complete Technical System
metadata.description:
A comprehensive technical system for formal methods in distributed systems, covering mathematical foundations, process calculi, model taxonomy, verification techniques, and industrial case studies.

This work presents 95+ technical documents with 550+ formal definitions, 380+ theorems/lemmas, and 450+ visual diagrams. It serves as both a theoretical reference and practical guide for researchers and engineers working with distributed systems formalization, including stream processing, workflow systems, and cloud-native applications.
metadata.notes: This is part of the AnalysisDataFlow project (https://github.com/luyanfeng/AnalysisDataFlow). The complete project includes additional modules on Flink streaming systems, knowledge graphs, and practical tutorials.
references: Akidau, T., et al. (2015). The Dataflow Model. PVLDB, 8(12).
Hoare, C. A. R. (1978). Communicating Sequential Processes. CACM, 21(8).
Milner, R. (1999). Communicating and Mobile Systems: The π-calculus. Cambridge University Press.
Kleppmann, M. (2017). Designing Data-Intensive Applications. O'Reilly.

files.description: Primary distribution files for the formal-methods module

types.description: Complete PDF version of all technical documents
ZIP archive of markdown source files

funding.note: This project is independently developed without external funding.
